mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months where FEDFUNDS declines, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0%, consumer spending power collapses and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, causing sustained negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in >60% of periods where UNRATE exceeds 9.0%, or the signal shows no statistical edge (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts signal broad-based consumer demand expansion, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive change in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 20-day returns with an accuracy above 55%, or the interaction term loses significance when added to a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days when inflation remains subdued.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during periods where CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 2.5% annually, or the 5-day directional accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation exceeds 2.5%, markets price in aggressive monetary tightening and margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL YoY growth exceeds 2.5%, or the threshold signal yields a negative Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding labor demand and rising household income, which supports near-term equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in >58% of observations, or the signal predictive power vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth combined with stable or declining CPI inflation creates a favorable profit environment, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combination of rising GDPC1 and falling CPIAUCSL fails to produce positive 60-day returns in >50% of quarters, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a period of elevated federal funds rates signals a supply-demand mismatch and tightening financial conditions, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during high-rate periods are followed by positive 20-day returns in >60% of cases, or the joint signal shows no incremental predictive value over FEDFUNDS alone.
